title: Dense Tunisian Sake Tagine
description: Resinous, dense, and comforting, this Tunisian dish features sake and is air-fried, making it a great option for those with FODMAP intolerance. Perfect for a chilly evening.

ingredients.name: Sake
ingredients.notes: Japanese rice wine, or substitute with dry white wine.

ingredients.name: Chickpeas
ingredients.notes: Rinsed and drained for a tender texture.

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together ghee, onion, ginger, and garlic to create a flavorful mixture.
Transfer the mixture to the air-fryer basket, and cook at 400°F (200°C) for 10 minutes, shaking the basket halfway through.
While the mixture cooks, mix sake, canned tomatoes, chickpeas, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper in a separate bowl.
Once the mixture is lightly browned, pour the sake mixture over the cooked onion mixture, and stir to combine.
Return the air-fryer basket to the device, and cook for an additional 5 minutes at 400°F (200°C).
Remove the tagine from the air fryer, and garnish with chopped fresh parsley, if desired.
Serve warm over couscous or crusty bread for a hearty meal.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed before serving.
Note: Traditionally, a tagine is cooked in a clay pot with a conical lid, but the air fryer provides a similar result with much less effort and a fraction of the time.
Tagine is a thick stew that's perfect for a chilly evening, and this recipe makes 6 generous servings.
Adjust cooking time as needed based on the size of your air fryer and the desired level of tenderness.
Cook time can be reduced for a thinner, more brothy consistency.
For a more intense flavor, cook the mixture for 15-20 minutes, or until the liquid has almost completely evaporated.
Experiment with different spice combinations and ingredients to make this recipe your own, and enjoy the rich, comforting flavors of Tunisia in every bite.
